---
title: "Data Model"
---

The directory comprises three linked data files: initiatives, people, and publications, stored as JSON arrays and connected by overlapping keys.

## Initiative record

Each confirmed initiative is recorded with: name, the source(s) it was found in, a plain-language description drawn from its own documentation where available, any stated institutional or funding affiliation, and the people named in connection with it. Fields with no available information are left blank rather than inferred.

## Person record

A person enters the directory because a confirmed initiative names them, not through an independent search. Each person is recorded with their name as given, and, where findable, an ORCID iD or OpenAlex author ID used to disambiguate them from anyone sharing their name. Institutional affiliation history and self-described research interests are added from ORCID, WikiData, and any profile a person has linked themselves, never from unverified matching.

## Publication record

Publications connected to a confirmed initiative or its people, structured as a custom extension of BibLaTeX. Populated from OpenAlex and Crossref metadata directly, or, where metadata is incomplete, from full text via GROBID.

## Confirmation status

Every initiative, person, and publication carries a status field: confirmed (has its own documentation, or independent mention in two of the four sources) or unconfirmed (found once, not yet independently verified). Unconfirmed entries are retained and published, not discarded, as a visible account of what the pipeline has found but not yet verified.

## What this model deliberately does not include

There is no assumption that every initiative will have equally complete data. An initiative found only through a funding record may have no stated membership; one found through its own working-group page may have full membership but no funding data. The model records what's actually knowable per source rather than filling gaps with inference.
